steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes a similarity between the two movies. The notes establish that both The Dungeon (1922) and The Fighting Ranger (1925) are lost silent films, and that silent films can be valuable historical documents. A strong answer puts both films together in one statement that highlights what they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *The Dungeon* (1922) was directed by Oscar Micheaux, and *The Fighting Ranger* (1925) was made by Jay Marchant.: Lists both films but emphasizes a difference (different directors), not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice B — Archives such as the Pacific Film Archive in Berkeley, California, play an integral part in preserving some of the surviving silent films from the 1920s.: Discusses archives, not the two films; neither film is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — Jay Marchant's 1925 film *The Fighting Ranger* is now considered lost, meaning no copies are known to exist.: Covers only one of the two films and therefore cannot show a similarity between both. ✗
  • Choice D — *The Dungeon* (1922) and *The Fighting Ranger* (1925) would be valuable historical documents of the era, but sadly both silent films are lost.: Names both films, invokes the note about historical value, and states the shared fact that both are lost — emphasizing their similarity on two levels. ✓
D is correct.
